entity.name: Tağmaç Çankaya
entity.description: Independent maintainer in Lefkoşa, Cyprus (Republic of Cyprus / EU citizen). I build small, commons-aligned AGPLv3 tools for solo operators and small teams: Echo Toolkit (discovery), Cairn (token-efficient multi-agent coordination), and Khashif. A consistent discipline across fifteen years of civic, environmental, and digital work: efficiency through configuration, not brute force; ship something concrete and let it endure.

projects.name: Echo Toolkit
projects.description: A free AGPLv3 toolkit that lets any solo operator, researcher, or small team build their own autonomous discovery agent. It harvests sources across the open web, classifies and scores them with a rotating cascade of free-tier models (zero marginal cost), runs a bounded multi-hop crawl, and formulates cross-domain intersections — where two unrelated signals suggest an opportunity neither could surface alone. Echo is the open-source engine beneath a private discovery agent (Khashif), shipped so the architecture is reusable: the free-tier cost-cascade, the intersection primitive, pluggable storage and outbound adapters (sqlite/Supabase, GitHub issues/Resend), and an echo/return-listening layer. Submitted to NLnet NGI Zero Commons (code 2026-06-2f6). FLOSS/fund support would fund the v1 public build: the discovery and crawl engine, the intersection engine, reference adapters, and documentation so downstream operators can run their own discovery substrate.

channels.description: EU (Cyprus) bank transfer. Account details provided on request to the maintainer.

plans.name: Echo Toolkit v1 development grant
plans.description: Fund ~5 months of solo engineering: the discovery + multi-hop crawl engine, the free-tier cost-cascade, the intersection-intelligence primitive, reference storage/outbound adapters (sqlite+Supabase, GitHub issues+Resend), a demo app, and documentation. Milestone-deliverable.
